760414:
- Oh Hyeon-Gu came off the bench to score the winner in the 79th minute after Korea fought back from being down 1-0
- Czechia's only real chance all game was Krejcí's header that actually put them ahead, but Korea just wouldn't stop attacking and wore them down
- Korea looked way sharper and had way more chances, so coming back to win felt inevitable even when they were down
760415:
- Mexico finally broke their curse with opening game wins — Quiñones and Jiménez got the goals in a 2-0 shutout
- Absolute chaos with three red cards (South Africa down to 9 men), though Montes getting sent off at the end took some of the celebration away
- After getting torched in their last seven tournament openers, Mexico needed this W and they delivered it at home
760416:
- Cyle Larin came off the bench and scored in the 78th to equalize—Canada's first World Cup goal at home ever, which is kinda wild
- Bosnia went up 1-0 early on a corner kick header from some injury replacement guy, but Canada fought back in front of a packed Toronto crowd with Connor McDavid and Ryan Reynolds watching
- At least Canada finally got a point after getting blanked in their last two World Cups, so that's something to build on
